findings.title: PlayOverlay introduces hardcoded bg-black/50 in newly extracted shell
findings.rule: .claude/rules/styling.md, Design Token System: 'CRITICAL: Never use hardcoded Tailwind colors. Always use design tokens from src/styles/theme.css.' Enforced by ESLint rule 'design-tokens/no-hardcoded-colors' (ERROR severity).
findings.evidence: courseCardShell.tsx:84 — `<span className="bg-black/50 backdrop-blur-sm rounded-full p-4 ...">`. Refactor plan called for design-token-only; this is the new shared primitive that all variants inherit, so the token violation is now propagated to every card.
findings.recommendation: Replace `bg-black/50` with a token (e.g. add `--overlay-scrim` to theme.css, or use `bg-foreground/50` if light/dark inversion is acceptable).

findings.title: categoryColors map uses hardcoded Tailwind palette colors
findings.rule: .claude/rules/styling.md: 'Never use hardcoded Tailwind colors. Always use design tokens.' Cheat-sheet shows `bg-blue-600` -> `bg-brand`, `text-green-600` -> `text-success`, `text-red-500` -> `text-destructive`.
findings.evidence: Lines 36,38,39,40 use `bg-emerald-100 text-emerald-700`, `bg-amber-100 text-amber-700`, `bg-red-100 text-red-700`, `bg-purple-100 text-purple-700`. Only the influence-authority entry on line 37 correctly uses `bg-brand-soft text-brand-soft-foreground`.
findings.recommendation: Map each category to token pairs (e.g. `bg-success-soft text-success`, `bg-warning-soft text-warning`, `bg-destructive/10 text-destructive`, `bg-brand-soft text-brand-soft-foreground`). The plan called for unified design tokens across the visual language.
findings.status: pre_existing_but_in_scope (lines unchanged but file is being refactored as part of unification effort)

findings.title: Difficulty badge classNames in progress variant use hardcoded green/amber palette
findings.evidence: Lines 632-634: `bg-green-100 text-green-900 dark:bg-green-900 dark:text-green-100 hover:bg-green-100 dark:hover:bg-green-900` and the amber equivalent. Note that earlier in the same file (lines 198-200) the popover difficulty badge correctly uses `bg-success-soft text-success` / `bg-warning-soft text-warning` — so the tokens exist and the inline progress-variant block is inconsistent.
findings.recommendation: Replace with the same `bg-success-soft text-success` / `bg-warning-soft text-warning` tokens used at lines 198-200.

findings.title: Empty-state cover uses hardcoded blue/indigo gradient
findings.evidence: Both branches render `bg-gradient-to-br from-blue-50 to-indigo-100 dark:from-blue-950/50 dark:to-indigo-950/50` as the empty cover. These are raw palette colors, not tokens.
findings.recommendation: Use `from-brand-soft to-brand-soft` or introduce a `--cover-placeholder` gradient token in theme.css.

findings.title: Info-button overlay uses hardcoded bg-black/50 and bg-black/70
findings.evidence: Both info-button branches use `bg-black/50 backdrop-blur-sm ... hover:bg-black/70`. ImportedCourseCard.tsx:408 has the same violation.
findings.recommendation: Promote a single overlay scrim token (`--overlay-scrim`, `--overlay-scrim-hover`) — same fix as PS-001.

findings.title: Progress variant gradient overlay uses hardcoded black
findings.recommendation: Use `from-foreground/30` or a dedicated scrim token.

findings.title: ImportedCourseCard placeholder + camera overlay use hardcoded emerald/teal/black
findings.evidence: Line 270: `bg-gradient-to-br from-emerald-50 to-teal-100 dark:from-emerald-950/50 dark:to-teal-950/50`. Line 273: `text-emerald-300 dark:text-emerald-600`. Line 408: `bg-black/50 ... hover:bg-black/70`. Line 487: `bg-black/40`.
findings.recommendation: Replace with tokens: `bg-success-soft` for the placeholder, `text-muted-foreground` for the empty icon, scrim token for the camera/info overlays.

findings.title: Mixed brand/text-on-soft-bg potentially inconsistent
findings.rule: .claude/rules/styling.md: 'Soft badges/labels: Use text-brand-soft-foreground (not text-brand) on bg-brand-soft backgrounds.'
findings.evidence: Line 313 uses `bg-card/95 ... text-brand` — not strictly a `bg-brand-soft` background, so this is borderline. Flagging as LOW because `bg-card` is a token and the contrast pairing isn't exactly the documented anti-pattern.
findings.recommendation: Verify WCAG AA contrast of `text-brand` on `bg-card/95` in dark mode; if it fails, switch to `text-brand-soft-foreground` or strengthen the surface.

residual_risks: The new shared shell (courseCardShell.tsx) is the unification point for all course cards but ships with a hardcoded `bg-black/50` in PlayOverlay (PS-001). Future cards reusing this primitive will inherit the violation, defeating the plan's design-token-only goal.

ESLint rule `design-tokens/no-hardcoded-colors` is configured as ERROR. If lint is currently passing on these files, either the rule has gaps for `bg-black/50`, palette names like `emerald-100`, or these files are excluded — worth verifying.
testing_gaps: courseCardShell.test.tsx asserts presence of `bg-brand` (line 11,17,22,28) but does not assert *absence* of hardcoded palette classes. A regression test like `expect(html).not.toMatch(/bg-(black|red|blue|green|emerald|amber|purple|teal|indigo)-/)` would prevent reintroduction.
No test covers the `[@media(hover:none)]:opacity-100` touch-device behavior of PlayOverlay — only hover-via-click is tested.
